audit_items.proof_trace: KZG proof verification returns Result<bool>, but line 2195-2199 uses .map_err()? which only handles errors, discarding the boolean result. When verify_kzg_proofs returns Ok(false) (invalid proofs), execution continues and the sidecar is accepted at line 2219.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker sends P2P data column sidecar with invalid KZG proofs. The gossip validation at validate_data_column_sidecar_with_state calls verify_kzg_proofs which returns Ok(false), but this boolean is discarded by .map_err()?. The sidecar passes all checks and is accepted, allowing propagation of invalid erasure-coded data across the network.
